'''山武姥山貝塚'''（さんぶうばやまかいづか）は、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[横芝光町]]姥山にある[[縄文時代]]中期から晩期にかけての[[貝塚]]で、縄文晩期の姥山式（姥山 α' β' γ' δ' 式）[[土器]]の[[標式遺跡]]である。[[1956年]]（[[昭和]]31年）以来、6回にわたる発掘調査が行われ数多くの遺物が発見された。なお、男性2人、女性2人、子供1人の一家と思われる5人が、悶絶した状態で見つかったことで有名な、[[日本の史跡一覧#千葉県|国指定史跡]]である、[[市川市]]の[[姥山貝塚]]と区別するため、「山武姥山貝塚」と呼ばれる。
== 概要 == 台地上に形成された貝塚であり、直径約120ｍの範囲に8箇所の貝層クラスタが谷頭に面して形成された点列環状貝塚と環状貝塚との中間と位置付けられ、縄文時代中期から晩期にかけ、今から5000年前に始まり2500年前迄続いた村であったと推定されている。点在する貝塚が全体で馬蹄形をなしているが、時期によって貝塚の位置が移動し、最終末になると貝塚はほとんどなくなっている。貝は、チョウセンハマグリ、ダンベイキサゴ、ヤマトシジミ、ウミニナ、バイ、シオフキを主体とする半鹹半淡の貝塚である。
==周辺== 周辺には、中台貝塚、鴻ノ巣貝塚、木戸場貝塚、牛熊貝塚など多くの貝塚があり、東側に縄文時代の丸木舟が多数発見されていることで知られた水系である[[栗山川]]と、南側にその[[ラグーン]]であったと推定される[[坂田池]]がある（丸木船の出土例は栗山川の若干上流の[[多古町]]および[[匝瑳市]]に多い）。また北側には、表情豊かな人物[[埴輪]]が出土したことで有名な[[芝山古墳群]]（殿塚・姫塚）がある。
== 関連項目 == *[[縄文時代の遺跡一覧]]

'''山武姥山貝塚'''（さんぶうばやまかいづか）は、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[横芝光町]]姥山にある[[縄文時代]]中期から晩期にかけての[[貝塚]]で、縄文晩期の姥山式（姥山 α' β' γ' δ' 式）[[土器]]の[[標式遺跡]]である。[[1956年]]（[[昭和]]31年）以来、6回にわたる発掘調査が行われ数多くの遺物が発見された。なお、男性2人、女性2人、子供1人の一家と思われる5人が、悶絶した状態で見つかったことで有名な、[[関東の史跡一覧#千葉県|国指定史跡]]である、[[市川市]]の[[姥山貝塚]]と区別するため、「山武姥山貝塚」と呼ばれる。

== 周辺 == 周辺には、中台貝塚、鴻ノ巣貝塚、木戸場貝塚、牛熊貝塚など多くの貝塚があり、東側に縄文時代の丸木舟が多数発見されていることで知られた水系である[[栗山川]]と、南側にその[[ラグーン]]であったと推定される[[坂田池]]がある（丸木船の出土例は栗山川の若干上流の[[多古町]]および[[匝瑳市]]に多い）。また北側には、表情豊かな人物[[埴輪]]が出土したことで有名な[[芝山古墳群]]（殿塚・姫塚）がある。

== 周辺 == 周辺には、[[中台貝塚]]、[[鴻ノ巣貝塚]]、[[木戸場貝塚]]、[[牛熊貝塚]]など多くの貝塚があり、東側に縄文時代の[[丸木舟]]が多数発見されていることで知られた水系である[[栗山川]]と、南側にその[[ラグーン]]であったと推定される[[坂田池]]がある（丸木舟の出土例は[[栗山川]]の若干上流の[[多古町]]および[[匝瑳市]]に多い）。また北側には、表情豊かな人物[[埴輪]]が出土したことで有名な[[芝山古墳群]]（殿塚・姫塚）がある。

'''山武姥山貝塚'''（さんぶうばやまかいづか）は、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[横芝光町]]姥山にある[[縄文時代]]中期から晩期にかけての[[貝塚]]で、縄文晩期の姥山式（姥山 α' β' γ' δ' 式）[[土器]]の[[標式遺跡]]である。[[市川市]]の[[姥山貝塚]]と区別するため、「山武姥山貝塚」と呼ばれる。
== 概要 == [[台地]]上に形成された貝塚であり、直径約120[[メートル]]の範囲に8箇所の貝層クラスタが谷頭に面して形成された点列環状貝塚と環状貝塚との中間と位置付けられ、縄文時代中期から晩期にかけ、今から5,000年前に始まり2,500年前迄続いた村であったと推定されている。点在する貝塚が全体で馬蹄形をなしているが、時期によって貝塚の位置が移動し、最終末になると貝塚はほとんどなくなっている。貝は、[[チョウセンハマグリ]]、[[ダンベイキサゴ]]、[[ヤマトシジミ (貝)|ヤマトシジミ]]、[[ウミニナ]]、[[バイ]]、[[シオフキガイ|シオフキ]]を主体とする半鹹半淡の貝塚である。
[[1956年]]（[[昭和]]31年）以来、6回にわたる[[発掘調査]]が行われ数多くの[[遺物]]が発見された。
== 周辺 == 周辺には、中台貝塚、鴻ノ巣貝塚、木戸場貝塚、牛熊貝塚など多くの貝塚があり、東側に縄文時代の[[丸木舟]]が多数発見されていることで知られた水系である[[栗山川]]と、南側にその[[ラグーン]]であったと推定される[[坂田池]]がある（丸木舟の出土例は栗山川の若干上流の[[多古町]]および[[匝瑳市]]に多い）。また北側には、表情豊かな人物[[埴輪]]が出土したことで有名な[[芝山古墳群]]（殿塚・姫塚）がある。
== 関連項目 == *[[縄文時代の遺跡一覧]] *[[日本の貝塚一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kkbunka/b-shigen/03shiseki/yokoshibahikari01.html (横芝光町)姥山貝塚] - 千葉県ホームページ 文化遺産 * [http://www.echiba.org/pdf/kenrenshi/kenrenshi_035_3.pdf 横芝町山武姥山貝塚出土の縄文晩期浮線文土器群] - 千葉県教育振興財団研究連絡誌 平成4年8月

'''山武姥山貝塚'''（さんぶうばやまかいづか）は、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[横芝光町]]姥山にある[[縄文時代]]中期から晩期にかけての[[貝塚]]で、縄文晩期の姥山式（姥山I、II、III、IV式）[[土器]]の[[標式遺跡]]である。[[市川市]]の[[姥山貝塚]]と区別するため、「山武姥山貝塚」と呼ばれる[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 縄文時代遺跡] 横芝光町ホームページ。 {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba| lat_deg = 35| lat_min = 40| lat_sec =08.4| lon_deg = 140| lon_min = 26| lon_sec = 39.9|label = 山武姥山
貝塚| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| width = 200| caption={{Center|}}}} == 概要 == [[台地]]上に形成された貝塚であり、直径約120[[メートル]]の範囲に8箇所の貝層クラスタが谷頭に面して形成された点列環状貝塚と環状貝塚との中間と位置付けられ、縄文時代中期から晩期にかけ、今から5,000年前に始まり2,500年前迄続いた村であったと推定されている。点在する貝塚が全体で馬蹄形をなしているが、時期によって貝塚の位置が移動し、最終末になると貝塚はほとんどなくなっている。貝は、[[チョウセンハマグリ]]、[[ダンベイキサゴ]]、[[ヤマトシジミ (貝)|ヤマトシジミ]]、[[ウミニナ]]、[[バイ]]、[[シオフキガイ|シオフキ]]を主体とする半鹹半淡の貝塚である[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/35/35948/70311_1_%E5%B1%B1%E6%AD%A6%E5%A7%A5%E5%B1%B1%E8%B2%9D%E5%A1%9A.pdf 報告総覧 山武姥山貝塚]] 奈良文化財研究所 。 [[1956年]]（[[昭和]]31年）以来、6回にわたる[[発掘調査]]が行われ、数多くの[[遺物]]が発見された[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html 山武姥山貝塚 - ちば観光ナビ] 千葉県公式観光サイト。 == 周辺 == 周辺には、中台貝塚、鴻ノ巣貝塚、木戸場貝塚、牛熊貝塚など多くの貝塚があり、東側に縄文時代の[[丸木舟]]が多数発見されていることで知られた水系である[[栗山川]]と、南側にその[[ラグーン]]であったと推定される[[坂田池]]がある（丸木舟の出土例は栗山川の若干上流の[[多古町]]および[[匝瑳市]]に多い）。また、北側には表情豊かな人物[[埴輪]]が出土したことで有名な[[芝山古墳群]]（殿塚・姫塚）がある。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[縄文時代の遺跡一覧]] * [[千葉県の貝塚の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * {{Cite journal|和書|author=鈴木公雄 |date=1963-08 |url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067 |title=千葉県山武郡横芝町姥山山武姥山貝塚の晩期繩文土器に就いて |journal=史学 |ISSN=0386-9334 |publisher=慶応義塾大学 三田史学会 |volume=36 |issue=1 |pages=67-94 |id={{CRID|1050845763875660672}}}} {{考古学}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さんふうはやまかいつか}} [[Category:日本の貝塚]] [[Category:日本の標式遺跡]] [[Category:千葉県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:横芝光町の歴史]]

'''山武姥山貝塚'''（さんぶうばやまかいづか）は、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[横芝光町]]姥山にある[[縄文時代]]中期から晩期にかけての[[貝塚]]で、縄文晩期の姥山式（姥山I、II、III、IV式）[[土器]]の[[標式遺跡]]である。[[市川市]]の[[姥山貝塚]]と区別するため、「山武姥山貝塚」と呼ばれる[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 縄文時代遺跡] 横芝光町ホームページ。 {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba| lat_deg = 35| lat_min = 40| lat_sec =08.4| lon_deg = 140| lon_min = 26| lon_sec = 39.9|label = 山武姥山

'''山武姥山貝塚'''（さんぶうばやまかいづか）は、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[横芝光町]]姥山にある[[縄文時代]]中期から晩期にかけての[[貝塚]]で、縄文晩期の姥山式（姥山I、II、III、IV式）[[土器]]の[[標式遺跡]]である。[[市川市]]の[[姥山貝塚]]と区別するため、「山武姥山貝塚」と呼ばれる[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 縄文時代遺跡] 横芝光町ホームページ。 {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba| lat_deg = 35| lat_min = 40| lat_sec =08.4| lon_deg = 140| lon_min = 26| lon_sec = 39.9|label = 山武姥山

To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as t..."
revision.text:
'''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Archaeology}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]]

revision.text:
'''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Archaeology}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:New pages]][[ja:山武姥山貝塚]]

'''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Archaeology}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]]

To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Archaeology}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]]

revision.text:
'''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Archaeology}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]]

[[Category:Archaeological sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sanbu Ubayama Shell '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Archaeology}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type sites]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]]

'''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama

Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late revision.text:
'''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Archaeology}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type sites]] '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Archaeology}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type sites]] The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟|12=draft}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市|12=draft}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市|12=draft}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟|12=draft}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市|12=draft}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|12=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type sites]] The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟|12=draft}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市|12=draft}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also ==
