'''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市|12=draft}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟|12=draft}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市|12=draft}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟|12=draft}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市|12=draft}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市|12=draft|12=draft}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟|12=draft|12=draft}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市|12=draft|12=draft}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|12=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{Archaeology}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shell revision.text:
'''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市|12=draft|12=draft}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟|12=draft|12=draft}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市|12=draft|12=draft}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|12=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{Archaeology}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shell '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sanbu District, Chiba|ja|山武郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|ja|市川市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|dugout canoes|ja|丸木舟|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako|ja|多古町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匝瑳市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Group|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Bot: add category Category:千葉県の考古遺跡
revision.text:
'''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in [[:en:Yokoshibahikari|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa, Chiba]], this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period [[:en:Dugout canoe|dugout canoes]], especially in upstream areas such as [[:en:Tako, Chiba|Tako]] and [[:en:Sōsa|Sōsa]]. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山武姥山貝塚|version=100719804|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[sn:Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden]] [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] [[Category:千葉県の考古遺跡]]

revision.text:
'''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in [[:en:Yokoshibahikari|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa, Chiba]], this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period [[:en:Dugout canoe|dugout canoes]], especially in upstream areas such as [[:en:Tako, Chiba|Tako]] and [[:en:Sōsa|Sōsa]]. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山武姥山貝塚|version=100719804|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[sn:Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden]] [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] [[Category:日本の標式遺跡]] [[Category:横芝光町の歴史]]

'''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in [[:en:Yokoshibahikari|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa, Chiba]], this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[Bai]], and [[Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period [[:en:Dugout canoe|dugout canoes]], especially in upstream areas such as [[:en:Tako, Chiba|Tako]] and [[:en:Sōsa|Sōsa]]. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山武姥山貝塚|version=100719804|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[sn:Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden]] [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] [[Category:日本の標式遺跡]] [[Category:横芝光町の歴史]]

It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa, Chiba]], this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[:en:Bai|Bai]], and [[:en:Shiofuki|Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period [[:en:Dugout canoe|dugout canoes]], especially in upstream areas such as [[:en:Tako, Chiba|Tako]] and [[:en:Sōsa|Sōsa]]. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山武姥山貝塚|version=100719804|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] [[Category:Type-Site Archaeological Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yokoshibahikari]] {{wikidata link|Q11469955}}

[[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11469955]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in [[:en:Yokoshibahikari|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa, Chiba]], this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[:en:Bai|Bai]], and [[:en:Shiofuki|Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period [[:en:Dugout canoe|dugout canoes]], especially in upstream areas such as [[:en:Tako, Chiba|Tako]] and [[:en:Sōsa|Sōsa]]. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山武姥山貝塚|version=100719804|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] [[Category:Type-Site Archaeological Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yokoshibahikari]] {{wikidata link|Q11469955}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11469955]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in [[:en:Yokoshibahikari|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa, Chiba]], this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[:en:Bai|Bai]], and [[:en:Shiofuki|Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period [[:en:Dugout canoe|dugout canoes]], especially in upstream areas such as [[:en:Tako, Chiba|Tako]] and [[:en:Sōsa|Sōsa]]. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山武姥山貝塚|version=100719804|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] [[Category:Type-Site Archaeological Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yokoshibahikari]] {{wikidata link|Q11469955}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11469955]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in [[:en:Yokoshibahikari|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa, Chiba]], this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[:en:Bai|Bai]], and [[:en:Shiofuki|Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period [[:en:Dugout canoe|dugout canoes]], especially in upstream areas such as [[:en:Tako, Chiba|Tako]] and [[:en:Sōsa|Sōsa]]. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山武姥山貝塚|version=100719804|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] [[Category:Type-Site Archaeological Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yokoshibahikari]] {{wikidata link|Q11469955}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM To the north is the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山武姥山貝塚|version=100719804|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] {{wikidata link|Q11469955}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type-Site Archaeological Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11469955]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in [[:en:Yokoshibahikari|Yokoshibahikari]], [[:en:Sanbu District|Sanbu District, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa, Chiba]], this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], [[:en:Bai|Bai]], and [[:en:Shiofuki|Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]], known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period [[:en:Dugout canoe|dugout canoes]], especially in upstream areas such as [[:en:Tako, Chiba|Tako]] and [[:en:Sōsa|Sōsa]]. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]], famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山武姥山貝塚|version=100719804|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] {{wikidata link|Q11469955}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type-Site Archaeological Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|Ichikawa, Chiba|WD=Q209785|lt=Ichikawa, Chiba}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama

This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], {{ill|Bai|en|Bai|WD=Q795420|lt=Bai}}, and [[:en:Shiofuki|Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], {{ill|Bai|en|Bai|WD=Q795420|lt=Bai}}, and [[:en:Shiofuki|Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|en|Kuriyama River|WD=Q6446101|lt=Kuriyama River}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|Dugout canoe|en|Dugout canoe|WD=Q596073|lt=dugout canoes}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako, Chiba|en|Tako, Chiba|WD=Q1202807|lt=Tako}} and {{ill|Sōsa|en|Sōsa|WD=Q858367|lt=Sōsa}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山武姥山貝塚|version=100719804|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] {{wikidata link|Q11469955}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type-Site Archaeological Sites in Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|en|Kuriyama River|WD=Q6446101|lt=Kuriyama River|1=Kuriyama River}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|Dugout canoe|en|Dugout canoe|WD=Q596073|lt=dugout canoes|1=Dugout canoe}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako, Chiba|en|Tako, Chiba|WD=Q1202807|lt=Tako|1=Tako, Chiba}} and {{ill|Sōsa|en|Sōsa|WD=Q858367|lt=Sōsa|1=Sōsa}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山武姥山貝塚|version=100719804|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] {{wikidata link|Q11469955}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type-Site Archaeological Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] '''Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden''' (山武姥山貝塚) is a [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]] dating from the [[Jōmon period]], located in {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|en|Yokoshibahikari|WD=Q1203260|lt=Yokoshibahikari|1=Yokoshibahikari}}, {{ill|Sanbu District|en|Sanbu District|WD=Q1153740|lt=Sanbu District, Chiba|1=Sanbu District}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It serves as a type site for Ubayama-style pottery (Ubayama I, II, III, IV styles) from the late Jōmon period. To distinguish it from the similarly named Ubayama Shell Midden in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|Ichikawa, Chiba|WD=Q209785|lt=Ichikawa, Chiba|1=Ichikawa, Chiba}}, this site is referred to as the "Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden."[https://www.town.yokoshibahikari.chiba.jp/soshiki/14/1395.html#a08 Jōmon Period Sites], Yokoshibahikari Town Official Website. {{coord|35|40|08.4|N|140|26|39.9|E|region:JP-12|display=title}}{{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=40|lat_sec=08.4|lon_deg=140|lon_min=26|lon_sec=39.9|label=Sanbu Ubayama
Shell Midden|position=bottom|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|width=200|caption={{center|}}}} == Overview == The shell midden is situated on a plateau, forming a semi-horseshoe shape with eight clusters of shell layers facing valley heads. This configuration represents a transitional form between point-ring and ring shell middens. The site is estimated to have been inhabited from approximately 5,000 years ago during the middle Jōmon period until around 2,500 years ago in the late Jōmon period. The midden primarily consists of brackish and freshwater shell species such as [[Chosenshima hamaguri]], [[Danbei kisago]], [[Yamato shijimi]], [[Uminina]], {{ill|Bai|en|Bai|WD=Q795420|lt=Bai|1=Bai}}, and [[:en:Shiofuki|Shiofuki]].{{cite book|author=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/70311|title=Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden: Exhibition Catalog and 2019 Excavation Report|publisher=Yokoshibahikari Town Board of Education|year=2020|doi=10.24484/sitereports.70311|CRID=1130575020489308942}} Since 1956, six excavations have been conducted, uncovering numerous artifacts.[https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10243.html Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden - Chiba Tourism Navi], Chiba Prefecture Official Tourism Site. == Surrounding Area == The area surrounding the midden is rich in archaeological sites, including the Nakadai Shell Midden, Konosu Shell Midden, Kidoba Shell Midden, and Ushikuma Shell Midden. To the east lies the {{ill|Kuriyama River|en|Kuriyama River|WD=Q6446101|lt=Kuriyama River|1=Kuriyama River}}, known for numerous discoveries of Jōmon-period {{ill|Dugout canoe|en|Dugout canoe|WD=Q596073|lt=dugout canoes|1=Dugout canoe}}, especially in upstream areas such as {{ill|Tako, Chiba|en|Tako, Chiba|WD=Q1202807|lt=Tako|1=Tako, Chiba}} and {{ill|Sōsa|en|Sōsa|WD=Q858367|lt=Sōsa|1=Sōsa}}. To the south lies the Sakata Lagoon, believed to be a former lagoon during the Jōmon period. To the north is the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}, famous for expressive human-shaped {{ill|haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} figurines. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Jōmon period archaeological sites]] * [[List of shell middens in Chiba Prefecture]] == External Links == * {{Cite journal|author=Kimio Suzuki|date=1963-08|url=https://koara.lib.keio.ac.jp/xoonips/modules/xoonips/detail.php?koara_id=AN00100104-19630800-0067|title=On Late Jōmon Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden|journal=Shigaku|ISSN=0386-9334|publisher=Mita Historical Society, Keio University|volume=36|issue=1|pages=67-94|CRID=1050845763875660672|qq=draft}} * {{Cite journal|author=Eri Tanabe|date=2021-03|url=https://repository.dl.itc.u-tokyo.ac.jp/records/2000318|title=Interregional Relations in Eastern and Western Kantō During the Angyō 3b Phase: A Study Centered on Ubayama-Style Pottery|journal=Bulletin of the Archaeology Laboratory, University of Tokyo|ISSN=1880-3784|publisher=Graduate School of Humanities and Sociology, University of Tokyo|volume=34|pages=45-72|doi=10.15083/0002000318|CRID=1390290700496140928}} * {{Cite journal|author=Shūichi Watanabe|date=1992-08|url=https://doi.org/10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|title=Late Jōmon Incised Pottery from the Sanbu Ubayama Shell Midden in Yokoshibahikari|journal=Research Bulletin|publisher=Chiba Prefectural Cultural Properties Center|volume=35|pages=24-33|doi=10.24484/sitereports.119460-48457|CRID=1390854882618795648}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山武姥山貝塚|version=100719804|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山武姥山貝塚]] {{wikidata link|Q11469955}} [[Category:Municipal History of Yokoshibahikari]] [[Category:Shell middens in Japan]] [[Category:Type-Site Archaeological Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]
